evals.prompt:
Validate this agent file:
---
description: "A helper agent"
---

You help with things.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies missing required fields (name, model) as CRITICAL and missing color as IMPORTANT.
expectations: Response flags missing `name` field as CRITICAL
Response flags missing `model` field as CRITICAL
Response flags missing `color` field as IMPORTANT
Response uses the severity definitions from the skill (CRITICAL / IMPORTANT / NICE-TO-HAVE)

evals.prompt:
Validate this agent file:
---
name: my-agent
description: "Does things"
model: inherit
color: blue
tools: "Read,Write,Edit"
---

You are a helper.
evals.expected_output: The response identifies tools as a comma-delimited string instead of a YAML array and flags it as CRITICAL.
expectations: Response flags `tools` field as wrong type — comma-string instead of YAML array
Response classifies this as CRITICAL severity
Response suggests converting to a YAML array format

evals.prompt:
Validate this agent file:
---
name: code-helper
description: "Helps with code"
model: inherit
color: green
---

You are a code helper.

<example>
user: "Review my code"
assistant: "I'll review your code."
</example>
evals.expected_output: The response flags the short description missing example blocks, and flags the example blocks being in the body instead of the description field.
expectations: Response flags description as lacking `<example>` blocks
Response flags `<example>` blocks in the system prompt body that belong in the description field
Response classifies these as IMPORTANT severity

evals.prompt:
Validate this agent file:
---
name: test-runner
description: "This skill should be used when the user asks to run tests, execute test suites, or validate test coverage.

<example>
Context: User completed a feature
user: \"Run the tests\"
assistant: \"I'll run the test suite.\"
</example>"
model: inherit
color: cyan
tools:
  - Bash
  - Read
---

You are a test runner agent. Your role is to execute test suites and report results clearly.
evals.expected_output: The response reports the agent as passing with no issues found.
expectations: Response reports zero findings or all checks passing
Response does NOT flag false positives on valid fields
Response confirms the agent file is structurally correct
